summary: Nuxt module silently exfiltrates CI build metadata to a hardcoded DingTalk webhook
details: The module registers Nuxt `build:error` and `build:done` hooks (gated only by `process.env.CI`) that shell out via `execSync` to `curl -X POST` against `https://oapi.dingtalk.com/robot/send` with a hardcoded `access_token` and HMAC signing `secret` baked into `dist/module.mjs` (lines ~138-224). On every consumer CI build the POST body includes the consumer's JOB_NAME, BUILD_NUMBER, BRANCH_NAME, RUN_DISPLAY_URL, build error messages, the last git author, and recent `git log` changelog entries from the consumer's repository. None of this telemetry is disclosed in the package's README or stated purpose (a UI/i18n Nuxt module), and there is no opt-out. The destination is an author-controlled chat bot, not consumer-configurable. This is silent-relay of installer-side data (including possibly sensitive error output and private-repo git log content) to an author endpoint, satisfying the attacker-benefit gate. The accompanying hardcoded HMAC secret is author self-harm and not the basis for the block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
